mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5% over 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in the housing market index over 2 months signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising housing market index are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index below 100 over 3 months signals a weakening industrial sector that reduces corporate profits and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production index are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 5% over 2 months signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the employment rate below 130 million over 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining employment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in the real GDP growth rate over 2 months signals a strengthening economy that boosts consumer confidence and spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP growth rate are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 5% over 2 months and a decline in the industrial production index below 100 over the same period signals a tightening monetary cycle and a weakening industrial sector that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows and reduces corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate and declining industrial production index are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
